content.text: GitHub Actions

is the automation layer that lets Claude Code run inside GitHub in response to events like pull requests, issues, comments, and reviews. In Claude Code workflows, it is the piece that makes Claude useful for repeatable collaboration tasks such as PR reviews and issue-driven coding work.
What it does
GitHub Actions runs
automated workflows on GitHub’s servers

when something happens in a repository, such as a new pull request being opened or a comment mentioning Claude. Claude Code can plug into that automation so it can review code, answer questions, or carry out a task without someone opening the terminal manually.
Common Claude Code uses
Use case
What Claude does
PR review
Reviews pull requests automatically and leaves findings.
@claude mentions
Responds when tagged in an issue, PR, or comment.
Issue automation
Picks up assigned or newly created issues and works on them.
Workflow tasks
Runs scripted development tasks from a YAML workflow file.
How it fits with Claude Code
Claude Code itself is the coding agent. GitHub Actions is the
execution environment
that triggers it at the right time. Together they let teams automate parts of the development lifecycle, especially review and issue handling, using the repository’s existing GitHub workflow system.
Setup and cost model
This integration is typically configured by installing the GitHub app and adding workflow files to the repo. Usage is
not the same as a Claude subscription
; it is generally pay-as-you-go through the configured API key, and it also consumes GitHub Actions minutes.
Why it matters
This matters because it moves Claude Code from a one-off assistant into a
repeatable team automation tool
. Instead of manually asking for reviews or task execution every time, the repo can trigger Claude automatically when the right event happens.
Mental model
Think of GitHub Actions as the
event trigger
and Claude Code as the
worker
. GitHub notices something happened in the repo, then starts a workflow that asks Claude to review, explain, or act on the code.
Key takeaways
GitHub Actions automates Claude Code inside GitHub workflows.
It is commonly used for PR review and issue/comment-triggered tasks.
It runs from YAML workflow files on GitHub’s infrastructure.
Costs can include both API usage and GitHub Actions minutes.
It is best viewed as the orchestration layer around Claude Code, not a replacement for it.
